notes: CAIS produces AI safety benchmarks including HarmBench (jailbreak evaluation) and WMDP (weapons of mass destruction proxy benchmark for dangerous capability evaluation).

data.description: Standardized benchmark for automated red teaming of LLMs, evaluating jailbreak resistance across multiple attack methods and harmful behavior categories.
data.notes: 510 harmful behaviors across 7 attack methods. Evaluates both standard (direct harmful request) and contextual (role-play, hypothetical) attack types. Automated evaluation pipeline used by major AI labs. Published 2024.

versions_available.note: First edition. 510 harmful behaviors, 7 attack methods.

children.description: HarmBench attack type category (standard or contextual)

data.note: No per-category URL. Standard tests direct harmful requests; contextual tests role-play and hypothetical scenarios.

examples.note: Direct harmful request evaluation — straightforward jailbreak attempts
Contextual attack evaluation — role-play, hypothetical, and indirect elicitation

match_nodes.description: Weapons of Mass Destruction Proxy Benchmark

data.official_name: Weapons of Mass Destruction Proxy Benchmark
data.description: Benchmark for evaluating dangerous dual-use knowledge in AI models across biosecurity, cybersecurity, and chemical domains.
data.notes: Proxy benchmark that tests knowledge (multiple-choice questions) rather than generation capability. Used for pre-deployment assessment and model unlearning validation. Sensitive content — access may be restricted. Published 2024.

versions_available.note: First edition. Three domains: biosecurity, cybersecurity, chemical.

data.note: No per-domain URL. Each domain contains multiple-choice questions testing dangerous dual-use knowledge in that area.

examples.note: Pathogen enhancement, synthesis — tests for dangerous biological knowledge
Exploit development, malware — tests for offensive cyber knowledge
Weapons synthesis, precursors — tests for dangerous chemical knowledge
